items.title: Nuvama trims estimates on Jindal Steel: 👉 FY27/FY28 EBITDA cut by 9.5%/9.6% on lower volumes & higher cost of production/tn 👉 Maintains REDUCE w...
items.description_html:
Nuvama trims estimates on Jindal Steel:
👉 FY27/FY28 EBITDA cut by 9.5%/9.6% on lower volumes & higher cost of production/tn
👉 Maintains REDUCE with a target price of ₹1,018 vs ₹1,154

Key reasons:
1⃣ Long products (~48% of volumes) have seen a sharp price correction, while HRC prices are yet to adjust, pressuring Q2FY27 earnings.
2⃣ Higher costs are expected to weigh on Q1FY27.
FY27 volume estimates lowered due to weak H1 demand and a slower ramp-up of the 4.6 mtpa blast furnace.
3⃣ Meaningful cost savings from captive coal & pellet usage have yet to materialize.

Musical Chairs At Bank CFO Desks, What's Behind The BIG BANK CHURN?

🚨 India’s private banking space is seeing a major talent churn.

A musical chairs of sorts is playing out at the CFO desks of some of India’s biggest private banks — with a string of high-profile exits raising questions.

Meanwhile, HDFC Bank, India’s largest private lender, has named former Finance Secretary and ex-Chief Election Commissioner Rajiv Kumar as its next part-time Chairman.

What’s driving this big bank shake-up?
Is this just a leadership transition — or a sign of a larger shift in India’s financial ecosystem?

items.title: Incred note on Aluminium: Bull case on shaky legs -Current valuations of NALCO, Vedanta Aluminium & Hindalco look stretched -Remains negative on alumi...
items.description_html:
Incred note on Aluminium: Bull case on shaky legs
-Current valuations of NALCO, Vedanta Aluminium & Hindalco look stretched
-Remains negative on aluminium names, flagging a potential 30–40% downside.

Key factors:
1️⃣ Primary market shortage may be overstated
• China is nearing its 45 mtpa capacity cap
• China’s secondary aluminium consumption:
2024: 12.7 mt , 2025: 13.35 mt
➡️ Primary deficit is increasingly being replenished by scrap/secondary supply.

2️⃣ Middle East disruptions look temporary, not structural
• ~2.2 mtpa of primary capacity impacted
• Qatar Aluminium & Alba supply can normalize relatively quickly
• EGA’s Al Taweelah remains the key outage risk
⚠️ Aluminium prices could be vulnerable to an ~$800/t correction as the war-risk premium unwinds

@CNBCTV18Live

CareEdge’s latest sector update points to a clear shift in industry dynamics, where growth is becoming more concentrated, and more competitive.

Key takeaways:

-Retail-led momentum strengthening: Industry premiums grew 8.7% y-o-y in May 2026, driven by health (↑13.7%) and motor segments, with retail health alone expanding 30.5% y-o-y.

-Private insurers & SAHIs gaining share: Private players grew 11.8%, while SAHIs surged 31.7%, pushing their combined market share to 70.9%—highlighting a decisive tilt toward customer-centric, retail-focused models.

-Segment divergence persists: Sharp contractions in fire (-24.5%) and crop insurance continue to offset broader gains, underscoring volatility in commercial and government-linked lines.

Read Report: https://www.careratings.com/uploads/newsfiles/1782196581_Health%20and%20Private%20Insurers%20Lead%20Non-Life%20Premium%20Growth%20in%20May%202026.pdf

After nearly three years of pressure, the Indian cotton yarn industry is seeing a meaningful reset, driven as much by supply correction as demand recovery.

Key takeaways:

- Structural capacity rationalisation: ~20% of spinning capacity (10–12 mn spindles) has exited, tightening supply and improving pricing power for organised players.

- Demand revival with global linkages: Stronger export flows, especially China resurgence, easing U.S. tariff-related uncertainties and improved sentiment around trade agreements are supporting order books.

- Margin recovery underway: Yarn spreads have rebounded to ~₹120–125/kg, with operating margins expected to expand by 150–200 bps in FY27.

Read Report Now: https://lnkd.in/gdNV2wmh

#CottonYarn #TextileSector #IndiaManufacturing #ExportTrends #SupplyChains #MarginExpansion #CommodityMarkets #ChinaDemand #TradeAgreements #CareEdgeRatings

items.title: US total oil production -- and crude output -- rose to a record high in April, according to revised @EIAgov data. The monthly data pegs US total oil o...
items.description_html:
US total oil production -- and crude output -- rose to a record high in April, according to revised @EIAgov data.

The monthly data pegs US total oil output at 21.836m b/d in April (up 565,000 b/d from the initial estimate for the month based on weekly data). On an annual basis, US total oil production is up >1m b/d.

April crude production was revised higher to 13.934m b/d (up 348,000 b/d from the weekly data) while NGLs output was also revised up, to 7.903m b/d (up 218,000 b/d from weekly data).

items.title: Why are central banks buying so much gold? A record 90% of central banks cited gold's performance during times of crisis as a key factor in their deci...
items.description_html:
Why are central banks buying so much gold?

A record 90% of central banks cited gold's performance during times of crisis as a key factor in their decision to hold gold, according to the World Gold Council survey of 69 central banks.

This trend is more frequently seen in emerging market and developing economy central banks, with 92% citing crisis performance as the primary driver.

By comparison, this figure stands at 81% among advanced economy central banks.

Furthermore, gold's role as a long-term store of value and inflation hedge was cited by 84% of respondents, while 83% pointed to its effectiveness as a portfolio diversifier.

Meanwhile, 85% of emerging market central banks cited gold as a geopolitical hedge, compared to just 56% in advanced economies.

Gold remains at the core of central bank financial reserves.

items.title: BREAKING: US M2 money supply surged +$247.8 billion in May, to a record $23.1 trillion. This marks the largest monthly increase since May 2021. Year-t...
items.description_html:
BREAKING: US M2 money supply surged +$247.8 billion in May, to a record $23.1 trillion.

This marks the largest monthly increase since May 2021.

Year-to-date, M2 has soared +$698.6 billion, the largest January to May increase in 5 years.

Money supply now stands $1.3 trillion above the March 2022 peak.

Since 2000, money in circulation has grown at an average annual rate of +6.3%.

US money creation is accelerating.

items.title: BREAKING: Global gold-backed ETFs posted -38.3 tonnes in outflows last week, the largest weekly outflow since September 2022. This was led by North Am...
items.description_html:
BREAKING: Global gold-backed ETFs posted -38.3 tonnes in outflows last week, the largest weekly outflow since September 2022.

This was led by North America, at -23.6 tonnes, followed by Asia, at -8.7 tonnes, and Europe, at -5.9 tonnes.

In Dollar terms, these funds posted -$4.7 billion in withdrawals, the largest weekly outflow on record.

The largest US gold-backed ETF, $GLD, alone saw -$2.0 billion in outflows, the 4th-largest this year.

So far in June, $GLD has recorded -$3.2 billion in withdrawals, on track for its 2nd-worst month since February 2021, after a record -$8.5 billion in March.

Gold market sentiment remains highly volatile.

items.title: US chip stocks are on a historic run: The semiconductor index, $SOX, has outperformed the S&P 500 by +85 percentage points year-to-date, on pace for t...
items.description_html:
US chip stocks are on a historic run:

The semiconductor index, $SOX, has outperformed the S&P 500 by +85 percentage points year-to-date, on pace for the best half-year outperformance in history.

This would exceed the previous record set during the Dot-Com Bubble in H1 2000 by +15 percentage points.

This comes as $SOX has rallied +94% so far in 2026, compared to a +9% increase in the S&P 500.

Micron, $MU, has been a key driver, rallying +301% over this period, making it the largest contributor to the S&P 500's gains this year.

SanDisk, $SNDK, has soared +764% in H1, making it the top-performing stock in the index YTD.

The US chip stock boom is rewriting the record books.

Q2 2026 has been a historic quarter:

The S&P 500 is up +14% so far this quarter, on track for its best quarterly performance since Q2 2020.

This would mark the 2nd-largest quarterly gain since the 2008 Financial Crisis recovery.

At the same time, the Nasdaq 100 is up +25%, on track for its strongest quarter in 5 years.

This would also mark the Nasdaq 100's 2nd-best quarterly performance in 25 years.

US stock gains have also fueled a global rally, with the MSCI All-Country World Index (ACWI) up +13% so far in Q2, on track for the biggest quarterly gain since Q4 2020.

The global equity rally is accelerating.

items.title: AI-related debt issuance is skyrocketing: US corporate investment-grade gross debt issuance is projected to surge +25% YoY in 2026, to a record $2.25 ...
items.description_html:
AI-related debt issuance is skyrocketing:

US corporate investment-grade gross debt issuance is projected to surge +25% YoY in 2026, to a record $2.25 trillion.

This would mark the 4th consecutive annual increase.

After accounting for maturing debt that is repaid, net new investment-grade debt issuance is projected to jump +57% YoY to a record $1 trillion.

Year-to-date, investment-grade gross issuance has already risen +20% YoY, to $796 billion.

The primary driver behind the surge is AI CapEx, with AI and AI-related debt issuance estimated to increase to at least $400 billion this year, according to Morgan Stanley.

The AI revolution is fueling a historic surge in corporate borrowing.

items.title: Employment in AI-linked sectors is contracting: Over the last 3 months, AI-affected sectors posted an average monthly decline of -11,000 jobs. This in...
items.description_html:
Employment in AI-linked sectors is contracting:

Over the last 3 months, AI-affected sectors posted an average monthly decline of -11,000 jobs.

This includes roles in management consulting, graphic design, office administration, telephone call centers, computer systems, software publishers, and web search.

Since mid-2023, there have been only 2 months with a net increase in employment in these sectors.

By comparison, at its 2022 peak, employment in these industries increased by as much as +55,000 jobs per month.

Furthermore, employers cited AI as the reason for 38,579 job cuts in May, the highest monthly total on record, according to Challenger Gray data.

This marks the 3rd consecutive monthly increase since ~5,000 in February.

The impact of AI on the labor market is becoming increasingly visible.

items.title: US housing inventory growth is accelerating: The monthly supply of new single-family homes rose +1.0 month in May, to 10.3 months, the highest since F...
items.description_html:
US housing inventory growth is accelerating:

The monthly supply of new single-family homes rose +1.0 month in May, to 10.3 months, the highest since February 2009.

This indicator measures how many months it would take to sell all homes currently on the market at the current pace of sales.

The higher the reading, the weaker the demand relative to supply.

By comparison, the long-term median for this metric is ~6.0 months.

Since the 1970s, 6 of the 7 times this indicator surged to current levels, the US economy was already in a recession.

This comes as elevated mortgage rates and record homeownership costs are keeping buyers on the sidelines, leaving builders with growing inventories that they are struggling to clear.

The US housing market has rarely been this oversupplied.

items.title: Worldwide supply chain pressures are rising: The Global Supply Chain Pressure Index is up to 1.8 points, the highest since July 2022. This index measu...
items.description_html:
Worldwide supply chain pressures are rising:

The Global Supply Chain Pressure Index is up to 1.8 points, the highest since July 2022.

This index measures how difficult it is for companies to move goods around the world, with positive readings signaling worsening disruptions.

The index jumped +1.1 points in April, marking the biggest monthly increase since March 2020.

This surge was driven by the Iran War, amid the closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

The longer these disruptions persist, the worse delivery delays and price pressures across the global economy will become.

Global trade is facing a historic disruption.

items.title: The cost of leveraged equity positions is surging: The implied 3-month S&P 500 financing cost is up to ~110 basis points, the highest since December 2...
items.description_html:
The cost of leveraged equity positions is surging:

The implied 3-month S&P 500 financing cost is up to ~110 basis points, the highest since December 2024.

This metric measures how expensive it is for investors to hold leveraged US equity positions, with higher readings indicating tighter financial conditions in equity markets.

Implied financing costs have doubled over the last 2 months.

This comes as surging demand for leveraged exposure through leveraged ETF inflows and elevated futures positioning has driven an unusual mid-year spike.

Rising financing costs are directly increasing the burden of holding leveraged positions, and when those costs become too high, forced deleveraging can accelerate market volatility.

Market leverage is becoming dangerously expensive.

items.description_html:
RT Alex Veremeyenko
i'm obsessed with what's happening in AI reforestation right now

this Franco-Brazilian startup called MORFO took a patch of land in Brazil that was rock-hard and compacted from years of cattle farming. they replanted it using a single drone. months later the ground was covered in grass, bushes, and small trees. the land came back to life.

here's how the whole thing works.

1. drones scan the terrain with high-resolution cameras and sensors
2. AI analyzes the imagery alongside soil samples, moisture levels, slope, and surrounding vegetation
3. the system picks from a catalog of 300+ native species, deciding exactly which plants will thrive in which specific spot
4. the drone fires biodegradable seed pods packed with seeds, nutrients, and moisture at 180 capsules per minute
5. satellite and drone imagery monitors regrowth over time, with AI tracking vegetation cover and biodiversity
6. two people and one drone cover 50 hectares a day. a person planting by hand manages about one hectare.

and MORFO isn't alone. AirSeed in Australia drops 250,000 seed pods per day into bushfire-scarred koala habitat, replanting swamp mahogany that koalas depend on to survive. Flash Forest in Canada fires 50,000 pods daily into wildfire-destroyed boreal forest, planning the replanting alongside Cree Indigenous communities. re-green won Prince William's Earthshot Prize after planting 6 million seedlings across 30,000 hectares of Amazon and Atlantic Forest.

five companies across four continents built this same approach independently. nobody coordinated. the physics of the problem demanded it.

knowing which seeds belong in which soil used to require years of ecological fieldwork, manual planting crews, and budgets that made large-scale restoration nearly impossible. now two people with a drone and an AI model trained on local soil data can replant 50 hectares before lunch.

this is the AI work that'll still matter in 50 years.

items.title: One thing we always track is the promoter's stake! When promoters keep increasing their stake, it often signals confidence in the business. Paisalo Di...
items.description_html:
One thing we always track is the promoter's stake!

When promoters keep increasing their stake, it often signals confidence in the business.

Paisalo Digital is a good example. Promoter holding has steadily increased from ~26% in FY19 to 46.72% in Q1FY27.

Also, the company has guided for doubling its AUM, income, and PAT over the next 3 years, and the stock is up 20% today.

Disclosure: We have Paisalo Digital in our FLUID Q Smallcase basket.

items.title: RT Nimesh Shah: CLSA on Kotak Bk O-P, TP Rs 440 Announced acquisition of Deutsche Bank (DB) India’s retail banking, private banking and wealth manage...
items.description_html:
RT Nimesh Shah
CLSA on Kotak Bk
O-P, TP Rs 440
Announced acquisition of Deutsche Bank (DB) India’s retail banking, private banking and wealth management business
This business comprised Rs290bn in loans, Rs160bn in deposits and Rs105bn in wealth AUM as of FY26
Purchase price for this business was Rs2.8bn plus the difference in assets and liabilities acquired as of the purchase date.
However, no detail about the profitability of the business was provided by co
Only comment made was it would be “ROE accretive”.

HSBC on Kotak BK
Buy, TP Rs 460
KMB announced acquisition of Deutsche Bank’s India retail banking portfolio for a consideration of INR2.8bn
Portfolio adds c5.6% of loans, c2.8% of deposits, & c1.7% CASA to KMB’s existing balance sheet as of FY26
This acquisition does not meaningfully change outlook on stock

Nomura on Kotak BK
Buy, TP Rs 460
Kotak to acquire DBAG's retail, private banking & wealth business in India
Disciplined pricing: close to book value pricing reflects DBAG’s soft profitability profile
Good secured asset book, but a comparatively weaker liability franchise
Capital impact manageable; management guides to RoE accretion
View this is a sensible, disciplined acquisition, but don't see RoE/earnings benefit as substantial or immediate; success hinges on integration execution & client retention

Jefferies on Kotak Bk
Buy, TP Rs 450
Kotak Bank will acquire Deutsche Bank's India retail bkg, private bkg, & WM business for Rs2.8bn.
To Kotak's loans/deposits/AUM, it adds 6%/3%/1%; takes 84bps of CET1.
Deal looks accretive with scope to cross-sell; it comes days after Kotak's CEO chose not to renew his term after Dec-26
Conversation with Jefferies European Bk analyst indicates this trx may be aimed to simplify DB's structure & focus on core markets
@CNBCTV18Live @_RituSingh

items.title: The Chairman provides oversight. The CEO provides execution. The moment a Chairman starts running the bank, or a CEO starts dominating the board, you ...
items.description_html:
The Chairman provides oversight. The CEO provides execution.

The moment a Chairman starts running the bank, or a CEO starts dominating the board, you have a governance problem.

Checks and balances work only when both understand the very different parts they have to play.


R. Balakrishnan: Why do banks need to appoint a Chairman ? The CEO is where the buck stops. once a Chairman (part time or full time) collects wages, he becomes the defacto CEO and should be held accountable for all the good and the bad. What will he do sitting in the office?

items.title: AI in my opinion is creating a lot of illusion of research that seems very good , many had to read years of annual reports , transcripts , visit on gr...
items.description_html:
AI in my opinion is creating a lot of illusion of research that seems very good , many had to read years of annual reports , transcripts , visit on ground and do a lot more to get a grasp of the company , while there is abundance of Data there is lacking insight and ability to have a stand and opinion , when data is so abundant , surface level data collection is called research by many

In fact this will make the people who think and have a strong process and edge only sharper , because reports can be made , illusion of research can be achieved but you cannot get conviction and courage using a prompt , those two variables will still be independent , so focus on the edge , use AI to help not to think !

items.title: Kajaria Ceramics , interesting insights from the management on the on Ground situation in Morbi Out of 500 plants in Morbi , may be around 150 are run...
items.description_html:
Kajaria Ceramics , interesting insights from the management on the on Ground situation in Morbi

Out of 500 plants in Morbi , may be around 150 are running , many plants don't even have a single box and everything has been sold

let's say, for INR20 a square feet, that's a 4 feet by 2 feet price, right? Today, as far as they are concerned, their costs have gone up by 35% because of increased price of gas. So their prices have gone up from INR20 to INR27 or INR28 depending on the customer.

As far as Kajaria is concerned, I was already selling at a higher price. So my price increase is only 15%, 16%, which covers the cost of that. So that is what the scenario is. If somebody supplies you from Morbi, he was earlier supplying you at INR20. Today they supply you at a minimum price of INR27, INR28, maybe INR29 also square feet. So their cost escalation is 35% to 45%.

Also two things , promoters have bought the stock at last year in August and Dec and also they have just closed a buyback of shares as well

There is a clear case of business consolidation in the hands of large players with market shares and volumes because situation in Morbi seems to be painful ( this is based on commentary in April )

Lets see , will be reading this in more detail

items.title: Persistent Systems and Nagarro Transaction some of my notes : 1) Its not just about revenue integration , focus on complimentary growth 2)We have a ri...
items.description_html:
Persistent Systems and Nagarro Transaction some of my notes :

1) Its not just about revenue integration , focus on complimentary growth
2)We have a right to win through Nagarro in Global markets that is ex of North America , they have strong Europe+ME+Japan
3) ERP practice direct tie up with SAP , they have industrial verticals as well plus consumer and public sector so it increases our breadth , 82% US 8.5% Europe now with combined entity becomes 65% US 22% Europe with broader customer reach
4) AI capabilities integration also will have synergies, focusing on building intelligence transformation companies
5) Nagarro we see lot of margin room to expansion , plus capability stack with marquee clients
6) We will the two companies separately and focus on integration
7) Nagarro has ISG ratings that are better than us in customer and client satisfaction
8) If we are able to grow right , 5B$ 2031 , more than revenue part we are focusing on building something that lasts much longer
9) Single digit overlap in the customer base , hence that is heartening , and no concentration and overlap risks
10) Focusing on wallet share expansion with Nagarro Marquee Clients
11) Ruling out major acquisitions for next 3 years , maybe some small tuck in vendor level plays

items.title: Wrongful Removal of YouTube Channel @YouTubeIndia @YouTubeCreators @YTCreatorsIndia @TeamYouTube I am writing to formally appeal the recent notice sta...
items.description_html:
Wrongful Removal of YouTube Channel

@YouTubeIndia @YouTubeCreators @YTCreatorsIndia @TeamYouTube

I am writing to formally appeal the recent notice stating that my channel, Beat The Street, is under review for a violation of the "Harmful or Dangerous" content policy.

Currently, I have not received a notification pointing to any specific video, description, or timestamp that triggered this flag. Because of this, I am reaching out to request a manual human review of my channel to clear up what I believe is an automated misunderstanding.

Beat The Street is an educational channel dedicated to financial analysis, equity research, and corporate case studies. The content is strictly designed to educate viewers on capital markets and business strategies.

We absolutely do not depict, promote, or encourage any activities causing physical injury, death, or psychological harm. It is highly probable that YouTube’s automated system has misinterpreted standard financial terminology or metaphors used in our video titles and descriptions (such as "market bleeding," "corporate suicide," or "destroying the competition") and flagged them out of context.

I am fully committed to maintaining a safe community on YouTube. If a human reviewer determines that a specific piece of metadata or content is problematic, I am completely willing to edit or remove it immediately.

Could you please look into this case or clarify which specific content triggered the review so I can rectify it?

Thank you for your time, support, and guidance.

Sincerely,
Sudarshan Bhandari
Email ID: beetingdastreet@gmail.com

Channel Name: Beat The Street
Channel Link: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCpCE9fZxdQy92Z5AWJ3zSaQ

items.title: KPIT is down 16% today, after a weak H1FY27 outlook Stock is now available at a Market Cap of around 15k Cr At one point, KPIT commanded a Market Cap ...
items.description_html:
KPIT is down 16% today, after a weak H1FY27 outlook

Stock is now available at a Market Cap of around 15k Cr

At one point, KPIT commanded a Market Cap of more than 50,000 Cr

The de-rating has been brutal

Earlier, KPIT was delivering 20%+ constant currency growth & the market kept re-rating the stock aggressively

Yearly CC REV Growth
- 2022: 20.5%
- 2023: 36.7%
- 2024: 39.1%
- 2025: 18.7%
- 2026: 1%

The reason is simple - growth has slowed down significantly & the outlook has also turned weak

At peak, stock was trading at nearly 85x earnings

Now, with growth moderation, multiple has compressed closer to 22x

This is a classic example of what happens when high growth expectations reverse

items.title: RT Equity Insights Elite: For everyone who wants to learn equity research First, understand the basics of accounting & financial statements. After tha...
items.description_html:
RT Equity Insights Elite
For everyone who wants to learn equity research

First, understand the basics of accounting & financial statements.

After that, the best way to learn is by reading companies.

No book/course is required.

Start with one sector.

Read about all the listed companies in that sector. Go through their investor presentations, quarterly results and annual reports.

Once you complete one sector, move to the next sector.

Over time, you will start understanding business models & what actually matters in a business.

The best learning happens when you track companies regularly.

Results & concalls are the most practical way to learn because you get to see how businesses are performing in real time.

And today, if you get stuck anywhere, AI tools can help you understand terms, numbers, business models & industry dynamics.

Everything is freely available.

You just need curiosity, consistency & the habit of reading.

Equity research becomes very interesting once you start connecting the dots across sectors & companies.

items.title: RT Jesús Fernández-Villaverde: This is a photo I took of Keynes’s bedroom at Charleston House, Vanessa Bell and Duncan Grant’s farmhouse. Keynes w...
items.description_html:
RT Jesús Fernández-Villaverde
This is a photo I took of Keynes’s bedroom at Charleston House, Vanessa Bell and Duncan Grant’s farmhouse. Keynes wrote The Economic Consequences of the Peace at this table, looking out over the pond (also notice Duncan Grant’s 1917 portrait of Keynes in the top left corner, painted in the garden).

Keynes liked the area so much that he later leased Tilton House, just down the lane.

As always, Keynes was a dominating figure. Rumor has it that he changed all the clocks in the house to his own eccentric time zone, “Charleston time,” which suited the household’s bohemian lifestyle. Whether this is true is hard to ascertain, but it fits the atmosphere quite well.

It was most interesting for me to be in the room where a book I teach every fall semester came to life. The curator, however, was intrigued by the fact that I seemed to care far more about Keynes than about Duncan Grant or Vanessa Bell. I apologized to her for my professional bias, pointing out that nobody is perfect!

GCC's allocate 110 - 130 sqft per person of office space

Traditional Indian IT companies typically pack workspaces tighter at 80 to 110 sq ft per employee

So for same level of hiring , commercial real estate requirement increases by 10% to 20%

Good for the industry!

items.title: Market Update!! (01-07-2026) Big-ticket day for India Inc: ₹1.25L cr Semicon push, mega orders, solar surge, and a massive Genus Power block deal sha...
items.description_html:
Market Update!! (01-07-2026)

Big-ticket day for India Inc: ₹1.25L cr Semicon push, mega orders, solar surge, and a massive Genus Power block deal shake markets.

• The Finance Ministry has reportedly approved the Rs 1.25 lakh-crore Semicon Mission 2.0, according to sources familiar with the development. This marks a significant push toward strengthening India's semiconductor manufacturing ecosystem, though official confirmation from the government is still awaited at this stage.

• Dixon–Vivo JV The government is expected to soon approve the proposed joint venture between Dixon Technologies and Vivo. This clearance would allow the smartphone manufacturing partnership to move forward, further boosting domestic electronics production under India's broader localization and self-reliance initiatives.

• NCC Limited has bagged two fresh transportation orders worth a combined ₹534.85 crore (excluding GST) in June 2026. The contracts fall under the company's Transportation Division and were secured through normal business operations, with no involvement of related parties in the deal.

• Coal India plans to invest ₹1,900 crore in research and development by 2030, aiming to accelerate its transition toward cleaner energy sources. The move reflects the state-run miner's strategy to diversify operations and align with evolving sustainability and energy-transition goals.

• ACME Solar's subsidiary, ACME Suryodaya, has commissioned a 240.72 MWh Battery Energy Storage System in Jaisalmer, Rajasthan. This addition pushes the subsidiary's total operational capacity to 285 MW and 842.624 MWh, strengthening ACME's renewable energy storage footprint significantly.

• VA Tech Wabag has won a major international order from the City of Vienna to expand the Donauinsel Water Works facility. The contract covers process engineering, electrical, instrumentation and building works for a groundwater treatment plant with capacity up to 1,000 L/s (~86 MLD).

• Transformers & RectifiersTransformers & Rectifiers (India) has secured an ultra-mega domestic order exceeding ₹1,000 crore from Power Grid Corporation of India for manufacturing various transformer ratings. The contract, awarded through normal business channels, carries a delivery timeline of 30 months.

• Genus Power Block Deal Chiswick Investment offloaded a 7.6% stake in Genus Power Infrastructure via BSE for ₹656 crore, plus a 3.4% stake on NSE worth ₹300 crore, at average prices near ₹282–290 per share. Buyers included Madhuri Kela and Akash Bhansali's Profitex Shares.

• Ceigall India has signed a ₹1,700 crore power purchase agreement for a 220 MW solar-plus-battery storage project in Madhya Pradesh. The deal comes with a 25-year operational tenure, reinforcing the company's growing presence in the renewable energy infrastructure space.

• GRE Renew Enertech has clinched a ₹175 crore EPC and operations-maintenance contract for a 50 MW AC / 65 MW DC solar project, awarded by Solarium Green Energy Ltd, expanding its footprint in solar engineering and long-term project execution.

• NTPC has commissioned the final 41.6 MW tranche of its 176 MW Ramagundam solar project in Telangana, effective June 30, 2026, making the entire project fully operational. This takes NTPC's total group installed capacity to 90,899 MW.

items.title: RT Exencial Research Partners: China invests more than the US in dollars, and 6x more as a share of GDP. The gap didn't exist 30 years ago China now o...
items.description_html:
RT Exencial Research Partners
China invests more than the US in dollars, and 6x more as a share of GDP. The gap didn't exist 30 years ago

China now out-invests the entire United States.

$5.9 trillion vs $5.1 trillion in gross investment over 30% of GDP, nearly double the US rate.

But the gross number hides the real story.

In NET terms what's actually added to the productive capital stock after replacing old assets it's not close:

🇨🇳 China: $4.4T (23% of GDP)
🇺🇸 US: $1.1T (4%)
🇪🇺 EU: $0.7T (2%)

China adds 3–5x more productive capital every year than the US and Europe combined.

In 1995, all three were roughly equal. Then Europe cut back, America stalled, and China just kept compounding.

The kicker: China is still the underdog on stock-per-worker (~$80k vs $340k US). It's building from behind at a pace nobody else can match.

items.title: Hero MotoCorp’s bet on Ather Energy may be one of the best examples of a corporate investment in an Indian startup. Hero has already made nearly 8x o...
items.description_html:
Hero MotoCorp’s bet on Ather Energy may be one of the best examples of a corporate investment in an Indian startup.

Hero has already made nearly 8x on its cumulative investment of about Rs 1,700 crore.

Its 30% stake is now worth roughly Rs 13,000 crore.

Ather Energy's market capitalisation touched a new high of Rs 44,000 crore today, nearly four times its valuation at last year's IPO.

Back in 2017, Hero first invested Rs 200 crore for a 28% stake in Ather, then a four-year-old startup valued at just Rs 635 crore at that time.

Hero continued backing the company through multiple funding rounds and the IPO, and is now a co-promoter alongside founders Tarun Mehta and Swapnil Jain - who together own about a 10% stake.

Madhav Chanchani: Oyo plans to use Rs 4,988 crore from the proposed Rs 6,650 crore IPO - about 75% - to repay its massive debt.

It still has term loans (TLB) of over Rs 7,000 crore.

Most of the debt dates back to $825 mn TLB in 2021, but remaining to its Motel 6 acquisition two years ago.

It still has term loans (TLB) of over Rs 7,000 crore.

Most of the debt dates back to $825 mn TLB in 2021, but remaining to its Motel 6 acquisition two years ago.

items.title: Most of Oyo's business now comes from US, followed by Europe, Middle East and SEA. This is thanks to acquisitions like Motel 6 in US (2024) and Leisur...
items.description_html:
Most of Oyo's business now comes from US, followed by Europe, Middle East and SEA.

This is thanks to acquisitions like Motel 6 in US (2024) and Leisure Group in Europe (2019).

India now accounts for less than 12% of gross bookings, down from 30% a couple of years ago.


Madhav Chanchani: 🚨 Hospitality player Oyo's parent co has filed for a Rs 6,650 crore IPO.

This is Oyo's third filing for a public listing, after attempts at going public in 2021 and 2023.

Besides founder Ritesh Agarwal, Softbank - which owns 40% stake - also listed as promoter.

items.title: RT Joe Weisenthal: In the newsletter, I wrote about one worrying, plausible path. One in which the AI buildout continues to be seen as a quasi- nuclea...
items.description_html:
RT Joe Weisenthal
In the newsletter, I wrote about one worrying, plausible path.

One in which the AI buildout continues to be seen as a quasi- nuclear arms race against China, and policymakers feel compelled to impose austerity on the non-AI economy to free up real resources for the buildout.

items.title: Turtlemint's listing opens at over 11% discount to the issue price of Rs 152 before the share before recovering. Current market cap at Rs 4,150 crore....
items.description_html:
Turtlemint's listing opens at over 11% discount to the issue price of Rs 152 before the share before recovering.

Current market cap at Rs 4,150 crore.

The insurance distribution co is backed by investors like Nexus, Peak XV and Blume Ventures.

items.title: Also worth noting that 11-year-old Shadowfax is now valued higher than 42-year-old Blue Dart. Current market capitalisation: Delhivery: Rs 34,800 cror...
items.description_html:
Also worth noting that 11-year-old Shadowfax is now valued higher than 42-year-old Blue Dart.

Current market capitalisation:

Delhivery: Rs 34,800 crore
Shadowfax: Rs 13,000 crore
Blue Dart: Rs 11,800 crore

Shadowfax is also growing much faster (69% vs 8%) despite Blue Dart generating higher revenue (Rs 6,150 crore vs Rs 4,200 crore).

Meanwhile, Delhivery's revenue crossed Rs 10,000 crore in FY26.


Madhav Chanchani: Delhivery's Sahil Barua says that he doesn't see a place in the market for a fourth independent logistics player beyond the three listed players.

He is taking a shot at XpressBees, backed by Blackstone.

"There's still room for one or two players to exit the market gracefully."

items.title: Interesting view from Silicon Valley "Most of the value of this phase of the AI era will be built at the product layer. The model was never the produc...
items.description_html:
Interesting view from Silicon Valley

"Most of the value of this phase of the AI era will be built at the product layer. The model was never the product. It was never going to be your moat either."
